food_name: Vanilla Lotus Gula Melaka Cake Slice

serving.natural_language_portion: One slice of cake, typically served as a dessert portion.

nutrient_source.title: Cake, yellow, commercially prepared, without frosting
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central for a commercially prepared yellow cake (FDC ID: 170902), as an analogous base for a vanilla cake. Specific adjustments for Gula Melaka and Lotus Biscoff are estimated based on their known nutritional profiles, primarily increasing sugar and fat content. Micronutrients not reported by this source are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake (average)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated based on the average GI of sponge cake from the University of Sydney GI Database, as a close analogue for a vanilla cake.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and processed food consumption
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this cake in moderation due to its sodium content (377 mg per 100 g) and as part of a balanced diet low in processed foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content, medium glycemic index, and refined carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Due to its high sugar content (34.6 g per 100 g) and medium glycemic index of 67, this cake should be consumed sparingly by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes to avoid postprandial blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sugar content contributing to overall dietary fat and calorie intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Consumption should be limited for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat (4.09 g per 100 g) and high sugar content, which can adversely impact lipid profiles and heart health when consumed in excess.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Vanilla Lotus Gula Melaka Cake Slice is a modern Singaporean dessert, featuring layers of vanilla sponge cake infused with the distinct caramel notes of Gula Melaka (palm sugar). It is often adorned with or incorporates Lotus Biscoff biscuits, adding a spiced, crunchy texture and a unique flavour profile. This baked dessert combines traditional Southeast Asian sweetness with popular Western cake elements and a trendy biscuit. Nutritionally, it is a calorie-dense food at approximately 367 kcal per 100 g, high in carbohydrates (52.3 g) and sugars (34.6 g) due to the Gula Melaka and added sweeteners, and contains moderate amounts of fat (15.6 g) from butter and cream. It is typically enjoyed as a sweet treat for afternoon tea or special occasions. Clinically, the high sugar and medium GI make it a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: Food name is exact, but nutrient data is adapted from a generic commercially prepared yellow cake from USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy), and GI is an analogous estimate from sponge cake in the University of Sydney GI Database. Multilingual names are constructed for a modern fusion dessert not present in primary regional databases.
